proj-kita-cover-a1b2c3-hero.query: Kita House is a private residence in the hills north of Kyoto. The brief called for a home that would dissolve the boundary between interior and garden. Concrete walls, left raw and unfinished, frame views of a centuries-old cedar grove. The plan is organised around a central courtyard — an empty room open to the sky. Kita House

proj-shiro-cover-p7q8r9-hero.query: Shiro Pavilion is a meditation and exhibition space set within a private forest estate in Hakone. The structure is a single white volume, its walls composed of hand-trowelled lime plaster. Inside, a sequence of rooms unfolds in near-total silence. Natural light enters only through narrow ceiling slots, tracing slow arcs across the floor throughout the day. Shiro Pavilion

proj-nami-cover-e4f5g6-hero.query: Nami Studio is a creative workspace for a fashion house in Minami-Aoyama, Tokyo. The design strips the interior to its essentials: exposed concrete ceilings, oak floors, and a single wall of floor-to-ceiling glass facing a private garden. Moveable partitions of washi paper allow the space to contract and expand with the rhythm of the working day. Nami Studio

proj-tsuchi-cover-t1u2v3-hero.query: Tsuchi Retreat is a six-room inn embedded in the rice terraces of Nagano Prefecture. The buildings are constructed from rammed earth and local timber, their forms echoing the horizontal bands of the landscape. Each room opens onto a private terrace and a view of the valley below. The communal bathhouse is carved into the hillside itself. Tsuchi Retreat

The buildings are constructed from rammed earth and local timber, their forms echoing the horizontal bands of the landscape. Each room opens onto a private terrace and a view of the valley below. The communal bathhouse is carved into the hillside itself. Tsuchi Retreat

proj-hana-cover-i7j8k9-hero.query: Hana Gallery is a contemporary art space in the Nakazaki-cho district of Osaka. The existing warehouse shell was retained and stripped back to its steel bones. New insertions — a floating mezzanine, a polished concrete floor, a single skylight — are minimal and precise. The gallery is designed to disappear behind the work it holds. Hana Gallery

proj-mizu-cover-x4y5z6-hero.query: Mizu Bridge is a pedestrian crossing over a canal in central Hiroshima. The brief was simple: connect two neighbourhoods. The response is a single arc of weathered Corten steel, its surface oxidising slowly to match the warm tones of the surrounding brick. At night, a line of recessed lights traces the underside of the span, reflecting in the water below. Mizu Bridge
